mechanism_narrative: A low and declining unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after a period of declining UNRATE is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, it signals an accommodative monetary stance that reduces discount rates and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicate broad-based economic expansion, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase MoM
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return when both series rise is not positive in at least 70% of cases over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-financial-crisis recovery regime, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals that the labor market healing is accelerating, which is particularly bullish for equities over the following 20 trading days as consumer confidence improves.
failure_condition: During the regime flagged as post-financial-crisis recovery, the 20-day forward return after a UNRATE decline is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index signals increasing inflation pressure, which erodes real purchasing power and raises the probability of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after a positive CPI print is not directionally negative in more than 55% of periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month growth in industrial production exceeds 0.5%, it signals robust manufacturing output that boosts cyclical corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return after a trigger event is negative in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in real retail sales and housing starts reflect synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, a powerful signal of aggregate demand that lifts equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST increase MoM
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return when both series rise is not positive in at least 2/3 of occurrences over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime defined by a federal funds rate at or below 0.25%, rising consumer prices are interpreted as reflation rather than overheating, reducing deflation fears and boosting equities forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than half of instances over any 3-year period.
